title: olearia floccosa
intro: Olearia floccosa, also known as the woolly bush or woolly daisy, is a flowering shrub native to Australia and New Zealand, characterized by its white or pinkish flowers and woolly foliage. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, flavonoids, and terpenoids. The medicinal properties of Olearia floccosa are believed to offer health benefits such as wound healing, antimicrobial protection, and anti-inflammatory relief, making it a potential treatment for skin conditions and infections. Olearia floccosa is often prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
